title: Comparing FCN Architectures
concept: FCN-32s, FCN-16s, and FCN-8s represent different levels of skip connections, trading off architectural simplicity for boundary precision.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What type of details do skip connections help recover?

options.text: Fine spatial details and sharp boundaries

exercises.explain: Correct! Skip connections bring high-resolution boundaries back into the decoder.

cards.title: The evolution of detail
cards.body: The original FCN paper introduced three versions: FCN-32s, FCN-16s, and FCN-8s. They are like versions of a digital camera. One gives you a quick, blocky image, while the others use smart connections to give you sharper, high-definition details. Let's see how they work.

cards.title: FCN-32s, 16s, 8s
cards.body: These architectures differ in their upsampling paths. FCN-32s upsamples the final layer by 32x in one go (no skips). FCN-16s combines the final layer with Pool 4 and upsamples 16x. FCN-8s combines Pool 3, Pool 4, and the final layer, upsampling 8x.

cards.title: Stretching a small photo
cards.body: FCN-32s is like taking a tiny photo and stretching it 32 times — it gets very pixelated. FCN-8s is like stretching it only 8 times, and filling in the gaps using a copy of the high-resolution original.

cards.title: Comparing outputs
cards.body: When segmenting a bicycle, FCN-32s sees a rough blob representing the bike. FCN-16s starts to outline the seat and handlebars. FCN-8s can resolve the thin frame and parts of the wheels.

cards.title: FCN-8s is not always the best choice
cards.body: While FCN-8s is the most precise, it requires more memory and compute because it processes multiple resolution streams. If you are segmenting large, simple shapes like sky vs. ground, FCN-32s might be sufficient and much faster.

left.text:
Upsamples 32x in one step
Very coarse, blobby masks
Fastest and simplest

right.text:
Upsamples 8x with Pool3 & Pool4
Highly detailed, sharp contours
Highest memory footprint

exercises.prompt: Which FCN architecture yields the most detailed boundaries?
exercises.explain: Correct! FCN-8s uses the most skip connections, preserving the most spatial details.

exercises.prompt: True or False: FCN-32s uses multiple skip connections to combine Pool 3 and Pool 4.
exercises.explain: False. FCN-32s has no skip connections and upsamples the final feature map directly by 32x.

exercises.sentence: The number in FCN-8s refers to the final ___ factor needed to restore the original size.
exercises.explain: FCN-8s combines features and then upsamples by a factor of 8x.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary trade-off of FCN-8s compared to FCN-32s?

options.text: Higher detail and accuracy vs. higher memory and compute costs
Smaller file size vs. lower accuracy
Slower training times vs. simpler architecture

exercises.explain: FCN-8s is more precise but requires more computational resources.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a segmentation model on an autonomous drone with limited onboard memory. It only needs to segment large bodies of water. Which model should you choose?

options.text: FCN-32s, because it uses the least memory and water boundaries do not require pixel-perfect precision.
FCN-8s, because it is the only model that can run on drones.
FCN-16s, because it is specifically designed for drone cameras.
A classification network, because we do not need a mask.

exercises.explain: Exactly! For large, simple classes like water, the coarse output of FCN-32s is fine, and its speed is perfect for edge hardware. Next: let's explore U-Net, the ultimate encoder-decoder architecture! Phase 8: Modern Object Detection is complete. Next up: Image Segmentation.
